content: Just as the double meaning of reflexion as both optical reflection and deliberate metacognitive reflection in human language learning lays the foundation for structured self-evaluation, LLM self-reflection mechanisms can borrow this dual framing to build a multi-layer reflection system: first reflecting initial outputs as an optical 'mirror reflection' of the model's current reasoning, then engaging in deliberate metacritical reflection to identify inconsistencies across outputs from different perspectives. This dual-stage framework can resolve the limitation that current LLM self-reflection often confuses shallow output restatement with critical metacognition.
